steps.title: Identify the slope from the downward trend
steps.content: The scatterplot shows a clear downward pattern: as $x$ increases from 0 to 5, $y$ decreases from 6 to 1. From point to point—$(0, 6)$ to $(1, 5)$ to $(2, 4)$—each unit increase in $x$ produces a 1-unit decrease in $y$. This is a slope of $-1$. Choices C and D both have slope \$6$ (positive), so they are eliminated.

steps.title: Determine the y-intercept to distinguish A and B
steps.content: Both A and B have slope $-1$, so examine the y-intercept. The scatterplot shows a data point at $(0, 6)$. Choice A: $y = -x + 6$ has y-intercept \$6$ ✓. Choice B: $y = -x - 6$ has y-intercept $-6$ ✗. The line passes through all points on the scatterplot when using choice A. That matches choice A — A is correct.
